beats.narration: Ultrasonic testing runs on a small set of numbers. Master three tools — wavelength, decibels, and right triangles — and every calibration comes easier.

payload.title: THE UT MATH KIT
payload.sub: The formulas behind every calibration and plotted flaw.

beats.narration: Velocity equals frequency times wavelength. The material sets velocity, the probe sets frequency, so wavelength adjusts: steel at 2.25 megahertz gives 2.63 millimeters.

payload.heading: The master equation: v = f × λ
payload.note: MATERIAL → V · PROBE → F · λ ADJUSTS

beats.narration: Decibels compare echo heights — ratios multiply, decibels add. Six decibels doubles the amplitude. Twelve is four times. Twenty is ten times.
Angle beams are right triangles: skip equals twice the thickness times tan theta. Sixty degrees on 25 millimeter plate skips 86.6 millimeters.
So do the math, then sanity check it against the part. A skip longer than the test piece means something went wrong.

payload.sub: A skip longer than the test piece? Something went wrong.
